name: auth_storm
title: Client Authentication Storm
description: 50 client FIX sessions attempt simultaneous Logon, overwhelming the broker's session establishment capacity.
background: In production, a network blip can cause all downstream clients to reconnect simultaneously. The broker's FIX acceptor must handle a burst of Logon(A) messages, validate credentials, allocate session state, and respond with Logon(A) acknowledgments — all within heartbeat timeout windows. If the acceptor chokes, clients time out and retry, creating a feedback loop (thundering herd). SRE must identify the storm, potentially throttle reconnects, and ensure order flow resumes cleanly.

steps.action: Verify broker host is stable with 3-5 active client sessions
Trigger auth storm: 50 simultaneous Logon(A) attempts from simulated clients
steps.expected_state: Broker session queue saturates; some Logons timeout or get rejected

steps.action: Monitor broker_host metrics: session_establishment_rate, queue_depth, rejected_logons
steps.expected_state: Spike in rejected logons; queue depth > threshold

steps.action: Check if existing sessions are affected (heartbeat delays, sequence gaps)
steps.expected_state: Some existing sessions may show degraded latency

steps.action: Implement rate limiting or staggered reconnect and verify recovery
steps.expected_state: Storm subsides; all legitimate clients reconnect; order flow resumes
